reviews.review_title: No hot knob gimmicks, only for real DJs
reviews.review_description:
I tried TribeXR... I'm actively giving feedback to Algoriddim's DJay...but this one had be immersed and re-living the glory days of vinyl mixing. No hot-knobbing here, just pure joy of mixing tracks, spindle twisting, an accurate simulation of pitch bend/nudge etc. The real deal.

What is solely lacking and thus I removedna star:
- it's 2025 and MetaQuest3 hand tracking is way advanced so should be implemented. It's a shame all the faithful reproduction of real world knobs and platter control has to be done with clunky hand controllers 

- there is no reliable way of using the clever stereo-to-mono out split for cueing on headphones as well as sending to live club speakers.

- no support for midi hardware DJ controllers. Even the lowly and portable Numark DJ2Go2 would be perfect (headphone and line out solved)

Hope the dev takes this feed back and works at the every essential least —implementing hand tracking.

New to dj'ing, thought i'd try this.

the sample songs have BPM in the information against the track, when you upload your own tracks there's no BMP, followed the instructions, and analysed the bpm using Rekordbox and uploaded a xml file to the same folder as the tracks, still nothing.

the instructions say navigate to the file on the pc and hit enter! there is no option on this - slightly disapointed

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for you review.

Loading Rekordbox collections is currently only supported on the PC version of Vinyl Reality. I have an update ready for the Lite version which adds this functionality to it as well, but can't currently release it because of technical reasons.

If you want to have BPM show up in the Lite version, you'll have to use an app that writes the BPM to the file's meta data, like MixMeister BPM Analyzer.

reviews.review_description: Everything works seamlessly and even helped me understand how to transfer files to and from the Quest. I currently have over 20 Gbs of music stored on here now and absolutely love filing through the "vinyl coffin" ! I would really recommend to the devs to add more button features such as beat-masher which adds a whole 'nother creative element to the playstyle and one of my favorite features in other djing applications. Other features I would recommend would be a flanger, a siren, a beatstop (music slows to a stop) , an automatic rewind, a phaser, a reverb, a delay, and maybe even a separate drum pad for fun (that ones a little out there lol). All in all, for a free application you can not pass this up if your even considering hoping on the decks at your next party or interested in learning mixes and matching bpm's and all that. As for me, i'll be spinning some Nujabes and letting the time fly! Cheers! 🍻
It would be amazing to mix music and see people in your living room at the same time.
This app really nails the feeling of vinyl turntables. Highly focused, it absolutely nails what it set out to do.
If you want to get into DJing this is the app for you. It’s very polished and true to the real DJ experience.

reviews.review_title: Very promising, and shocked it's free!
reviews.review_description:
Background: Been a DJ for over 25 years and started on turntables before switching to digital around 15 years ago.
Selecting music is easy (to see the track art on the label is a very nice touch!). All the main parts of the mixing process have been thought through, from cueing to nudging the deck for easier beatmatching. The EQ isn't blown out which is nice and levelled correctly. A VERY nice touch was being able to see the track breakdowns in the vinyl itself. Navigating through folders seemed a little tricky, the mixer could do with a couple nice effects added, and I couldn't easily pull of a spinback, but as it stands right now it's an easy 4 out of 5 and if some of the niggles get sorted, maybe some environmental features like some visual FX, video screens etc, this is easily going to be a 5. Very nice work!

reviews.review_title: Great app that is fun and easy to use
reviews.review_description:
I haven’t had any previous dj experiences, but after using this app for a couple hours I can say I’ve created some sick mixes and actually find myself loving my own mixes instead of cringing. It is a great app to experiment different songs and techniques. It is also visually appealing. 

It’s very fun and great to use.
That person was actually me when you weere chatting on discord! I forgot to update my review, the game is amazing and is definitely worth every cent after resolving the issue.

developer_response.body: After chatting on Discord, I think this issue was resolved by doing a factory reset of the headset?

reviews.review_description: I used to DJ in clubs 20yrs ago, we got our son the quest 2 and I came across this app. Unbelievable real like. I have techinic 1210s , I've just got figure how to unlock the rest.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your review.

Regarding your issue, did you allow the app permission to access media files on the device when prompted? If the permission is denied, the app can’t read the mp3 files which you transferred to the device’s music folder.

If the issue still persist after allowing the permissions, please get in touch directly through Discord, or via the contact form on the Vinyl Reality website: https://www.vinyl-reality.com/contact/

reviews.review_description: I just tried this free app for about 1 hour and I have to say, I am amazed by the quality and realistic way it works! I have to admit, that I am really new to VR, having bought my first VR set (Quest 2) just 7 days ago, but I have been DJing for 40 years now, using the SL 1200's and this looks and feels almost like the real thing. I still struggle with letting go of the record and it being in synch with the other record right away, like I'm used to with real decks, but that is just a matter of more experience with the controllers. The way you found a way of using the PFL by tapping the side is something, I really like! I also love how you can adjust the turntables and mixer settings, like IRL (fader curves, stop speed, etc) Unfortunately,I did not see a way of using my own MP3 collection, since I couldn't access Air Link with this free version, but since it was a test, the records that were supplied were ok to use. I was completely awe struck, when I read you are able to upload and use your own slip mat design! In that case, something I loke to see (as a Hip Hop DJ) would be the option to put the turntables in the so called "battle mode" (i.e. side ways, so they are closer together, next to the mixer) or even next to each other and the mixer on either the left or right side. :) I think I need to buy the PC version to use my own collection, so I will do this and see how I can perhaps start to use it for live streaming as well. :) Cheers, from DJ Bite, Amsterdam, The Netherlands

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the detailed review, glad to hear you're enjoying the app!

You can load your own tracks using the Lite version, there's no need to buy the PC version, just follow the instructions from this page:
https://vinyl-reality.com/wiki/doku.php?id=vinyl_reality_lite_music_files

reviews.review_description:
It seemed to have worked for everyone else but the tutorial doesn't work for me. There is no cd to grab in both game and tutorial. Everything else looks great though but I wish I could try it!


EDIT: I actually didn't allow for the media to be used. Thank you for helping me out! I tried the tutorial and it was simple and easy to understand. I never done anything with this equipment so this more for fun when I do it. If you need practice with this stuff or just like making music I would check it out!

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your review and sorry to hear you're having issues.

The missing records in the case could be caused by a couple of things:
- You didn't allow the app permission to access your media folders (needed to read tracks from the Music folder)
- You're not using the admin account on your Quest

If you need more support with this issue, you can:
- Join the Vinyl Reality Discord server: https://discord.gg/tQ7cNR4
- Or use the contact form on the website: https://www.vinyl-reality.com/contact/

reviews.review_description:
I have been djing for 15 years starting with the exact setup as in Vinyl Reality. Recreating this exact experience in VR is nothing short of incredible. 
If you have or have been spinning records just get this, you won’t regret it.
Didn’t enjoy this game at all, it works fine, but not a great pick up and play option.
